Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.util

Class Sets

java.lang.Object
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.util.Sets
public final class Sets extends Object
Set helper functions.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static <E> Set<E>
    difference(Set<E> first, Set<E> second)
    Returns the elements in first minus the elements in second, retaining the iteration order.
    static <E> Set<E>
    fromCollection(Collection<E> collection)
     
    static <E> Set<E>
    intersection(Set<E> first, Set<E> second)
    Returns the elements present in both sets, retaining the iteration order of the first set.

    Methods declared in class java.lang.Object

    equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • fromCollection

      public static <E> Set<E> fromCollection(Collection<E> collection)
      Type Parameters:
      E - the type of elements held by the collection
      Parameters:
      collection - a collection
      Returns:
      collection if it is already a Set; otherwise, a new Set containing collection's elements
      Throws:
      AssertionError - if collection is null

    • difference

      public static <E> Set<E> difference(Set<E> first, Set<E> second)
      Returns the elements in first minus the elements in second, retaining the iteration order.
      Type Parameters:
      E - the type of elements in the sets
      Parameters:
      first - the first set
      second - the second set
      Returns:
      a set containing the elements present in the first set but not in the second set
      Throws:
      AssertionError - if any of the arguments are null

    • intersection

      public static <E> Set<E> intersection(Set<E> first, Set<E> second)
      Returns the elements present in both sets, retaining the iteration order of the first set.
      Type Parameters:
      E - the type of elements in the sets
      Parameters:
      first - the first set
      second - the second set
      Returns:
      a set containing the elements present in both sets
      Throws:
      AssertionError - if any of the arguments are null
